english: Wayfinder,I found one of the Oracle Cards after our fight against the beast. Something about your presence must have drawn it forward. They're curious little objects, almost sentient...Thank you for the aid, and remember to come find me once the rest have revealed themselves to you.—Dagda

Wayfinder,I've been wanting to speak with you regarding the relics you helped R'tchikk recover in the Wizard's Tower. Come find me on the lower level of the Wizard's Tower, by the central staircase, if you want to learn more about them and how they might be able to help you grow in power.—Archivist Ikur

Wayfinder,It's the medic who checked you when you first got here. I heard about your run-in at the Bastion of the Obscure. How is your mind? Any dark thoughts? Let me know if you need help.Sometimes an item or some sort of personal effect can help you stay grounded. I've sent along a special card I've had for quite a while, but I sensed it was time to pass it along. I'd used it as a bookmark for so long, but I... Strange feeling, is all. I hope the gesture lands.Jonea

Wayfinder,One of our researchers in the Astral Ward has made a peculiar discovery—like you, she's a revenant. She's currently stationed in Lornar's Pass, supporting the Durmand Priory with the archival updates at their headquarters. If you'd be willing to meet with her, I'd appreciate it.—Frode

Wayfinder,R'tchikk works to repair the Museum of Curiosities after the invasion. Many trinkets are lost. Many statues damaged by fighting.But! This is also a chance to build new displays for the good of future friends and others. Meet R'tchikk and Gladium at the Wizard's Tower, and we will explain.—R'tchikk
Waypoints can be used to travel quickly for a small fee.
Waypoints can be used to travel quickly from place to place for a small fee.
Wazzir spends time atop the dunes with his choya friend, Pokey.
We are honored to have your support, %str1%. Your presence and aid in Doric's Landing continues to bolster our defense of the region. We look forward to seeing you again.—Lieutenant Bran

We are thankful for your help at Mafic Core. Destroyers have become a great problem for my people. Many have been called to action to fight the destroyer invasions. We must be vigilant in the face of growing danger. May our ancestors provide us with guidance and strength. —Sangdo Swiftwing

We attempted the Foefire cleansing ritual, which didn't go entirely as planned. Some spirits were drawn into the Mists portal, but so was Sohothin, Rytlock's sword. Knowing that a full cleansing of the Foefire was impossible without his enchanted blade, he leapt in after it.

Smodur agreed to attend the summit since the ghost problem, while not solved, was lessened by our efforts. He seemed confident that Rytlock would hold his own while trapped in the Mists, though Rox expressed worry.
We cats are all in this together.
We cavaliers are stretched thin across the region, struggling to protect our vital farmers, quarriers, and ranchers.
We delay too long. We must get this device ready to go soon.

We did it! The Priory at Landslide Lee have befriended the skritt in Kolkorensburg. We can now enter their cave and study the relics and crystal formations within. That's two things you've helped us achieve. This is an exciting day for the Priory—you have our gratitude and our thanks.—Antal the Patient

We find ourselves at the Harvest Temple, perched on the edge of history.  As Vizu's strike lands, time itself seems to pause, as if rendered a mere spectator of fate. It is here that Shiro stands, frozen. Unarmed. Vulnerable.  From opposing sides of the room, St. Viktor and Archemorus lock gazes. Together, they stride, side by side, two bodies choreographed by destiny. St. Viktor grabs the first of the blades. He palms the handle, still hot with Shiro's fury. Archemorus grasps the second. Each angles a blade toward Shiro's sides.  Shiro inhales. Closes his eyes. Waits. The pierce of the blades is instantaneous. Cold, yet white hot. Concentrated agony.  And it begins. Reality imploding into pure energy. Sight and sound blur into something indistinguishable: the sheer force of life collapsing upon itself. Existence is a vast wind. A deafening hum. A hue of jade green.  Then, nothing.

We found an old journal in the ruins nearby. And a key. It seems to be some sorta...vault manual? Probably looted by another thief trying to crack the code, too. Don't think they got very far."The door will only open by their will."I dunno who the heck it's talking about, but if we're talking pre-Zhaitan Disaster we're probably out of luck. The whole thing is pretty cryptic, honestly. I don't like messing with blood magic.Royals, always so dramatic.

We found one of the pirates, name of Drake, passed out and reeking of grog. I don't know how reliable his intel is, but he mentioned a charr and another pirate he didn't recognize, so I figure she has help.
We had our own vista with a view of the Bay, the market, the hylek, and the tengu.
We have a few more fish to eat and more smiles around the table because of you. Thank you, comrade.—Dredge Supplier Yuliya

We have allowed a significant amount of time to pass since last observing the progression of the Maguuma fractal, and the overgrowth we saw before has continued as predicted. The inhabitants of the jungle have grown in size and ferocity as they've adapted to these new conditions, and a sort of equilibrium among species has been reached. It seems that even under extreme conditions, life in Tyria survives.Conclusion: While ley-line instability still represents a meaningful concern, Tyria remains remarkably resilient. It appears likely that some version of Tyrian life would survive even in our worst projections. However, the experiment is worth continuing in order to study the long-tail effects.

We have capped the number of points that players can earn from a repeatable achievement at 100. We realize that this cap implementation may have changed your achievement point totals, but it was necessary to support an upcoming reward structure for achievement points. We've attached a bonus reward to help make up for some of your time spent gaining these additional achievement points. Thanks for playing Guild Wars 2!
We have chosen our path, though it is fraught with difficulties. Should you wish to aid our journey to victory, go to the Eye. Lend us your skill.—Still Waters
We have summoned a Mist Champion
We have to get out of this twisted place.

We hear him. His voice is soft like moss, yet sound as the rock upon which it grows.  Like a low hymn carried by the wind, it sings to us. Beckons us forth. We must follow.  The first steps toward the epochal end. "Fate has written it."  It is only a matter of time now.
We know you're one of them! Don't try to deny it!

We met them at the pass in Thunderhead Peaks: five thousand of our finest soldiers blocking the only way forward for the Stone Summit forces. We were outnumbered, yes, but the narrow pass would force them to fight on our terms, and our archers controlled the cliff faces. It was our best opportunity to stop their advance.    Had it been only the traitors, we'd have surely prevailed; their first wave crashed weakly into our shields and were cut down by our support from above. Then the mursaat appeared. I've seen many things in my day, but the horrors they inflicted on our forces will haunt me to my dying breath. Within minutes of their assault our lines broke, soldiers trampling each other as they fled. The mursaat killed most of us as we fled. But after what I saw that day, I think they might have been the lucky ones.
We must all do our part.

We must be getting close to something big here, because they wouldn't have left so many impediments if they weren't defending something important. The others express their doubts, but I've instructed them to press on. It won't be long now.
We must discover the truths that Tyria needs to survive – where the dragons came from, what they want, and how to defeat them.
We must unite the races against their destruction, or nothing in this world will be spared the falling of the stars. I choose to unify them by

We must unite the races against their destruction, or nothing in this world will be spared the falling of the stars. I choose to unify them by demanding their capitulation, conquering where they will not serve, and breaking them if they will not bend.

We must unite the races against their destruction, or nothing in this world will be spared the falling of the stars. I choose to unify them by showing through bravery and bold deeds that together, we can do anything.

We must unite the races against their destruction, or nothing in this world will be spared the falling of the stars. I choose to unify them by speaking to each race in turn, and convincing them to lay aside old grievances.
We need that gate! Finders keepers. Rata Arcanum is ours!
We needed to go back to the Coil and look for more clues on this "Mordremoth."

We regret to inform the people of Gavril that our guiding knight, so ardent, has passed; Justiciar Rhea, our eyes are on your back as you walk up the golden path. Her battle against illness was brief, and sadly, it was quickly lost...but every swing was radiant.    A memorial will be held for Rhea in the coming days. Listen for song, you'll know what to do.
We reset all demo characters. Delete and re-create this character to reuse its name.

We returned to the waking world to find Mordremoth still alive; its essence took shelter inside Trahearne, and there was no way to finish off the dragon without also killing the marshal. At his urging and with great regret...I ended Trahearne's life. Mordremoth was destroyed—once and for all. The dragon's death sent an enormous wave of magical energy across the entire jungle; it remains to be seen what effect this wave will have on the region, and the entire world.But that mystery can wait for tomorrow. Right now, we have more important things to get done: to remember those we've lost, to heal those who were hurt, to care for those who survived. The losses and sacrifices we've endured...this victory has to be worth the price we paid. We must make them count. I don't know what the future holds, but we must be prepared for whatever awaits us.

We sent three scout teams to survey the area, and none ever came back alive. Three was more than we could afford, but we tried our best to know what we were heading into. All we really knew in the end was that they were dug in and ready for a fight. We had to soften their defenses first; it would have been suicidal to attack otherwise. So we bombarded them with spells from a distance, just like the handbook calls for.    It was only when we got into the encampment and saw the bodies that we realized it had been a prison. They had been in cages when the fire came down, and there were claw marks in the walls where they had tried to escape. Many were still burning, a smell I doubt that any of us will ever forget. We cut down the loyalists who had not already fled—our anger directed at them to keep from attacking ourselves.    I don't know what fate awaited them if we hadn't been here, so I can't say whether it would have been better or worse. But we failed them here, and that's something we may never be able to atone for. We'll fight on, at least, because that's what they deserve. Maybe then this loss won't be in vain.
We were driven from the heart of the earth, forced to live in this newer, brighter world.
We will not be driven out again.
We will see you in the future in even better form.
We're barely keeping them at bay as it is!

We're catching up on our quota, and you've earned yourself a break. I'm impressed. You have either a promising future as a prisoner guard or maybe even as a prisoner. I'm not sure who's more useful to me anymore. —Warden Scourejaw
We're collecting donations to support Extra Life. Charity streams will be on November 4th. Click here to check the website for details. Thanks for your support!
We're collecting donations to support Extra Life. Thanks for your support!

We're continuing the tradition of supporting Children's Miracle Network through Extra Life Game Day, and we want to thank you for being part of our community! Enjoy an in-game boost to magic find, experience points, and more during this event.Join us on game day on our Twitch channel to show your support for Extra Life! Your donations support Children's Miracle Network—building awareness for children's hospitals and providing much-needed funds for children with urgent medical needs and their families.To become part of our Super Team, or to create a sub-team and join forces with us, visit www.extra-life.org/arenanet. Together, we can change kids' health!
We're glad for your help, newcomer. The Branded have made survival a constant struggle.You're welcome in our krall any time.—Groffmok

We're heading into the field again, Agent. Should be fun: a large army of crystallized creatures is gathering in the Brand, but we need more information before we act. We'll start with the ogres of Agrak Kraal—if anyone knows what's going on with the local wild beasties, it's them. Meet me there and we'll get to the bottom of this.—Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw

We're managing to hold out against the undead. It is, however, unlikely that conditions will improve. If I weren't assigned here, I'd gladly go south and face the threat head on.—Explorer Malcolm, Durmand Priory
We're not getting out of here, are we?
We're not just dealing with dark magic. This is something much more sinister. She needs our help.
We're sorry! Account Upgrade is currently undergoing maintenance. Please try again later.

We're taking the field again, Agent. Reports tell of grawl worshipping a dragon minion...I swear, those ape-men will worship anything, won't they? Problem is, they're making our mutual enemy stronger, so we have to convince them to stop. Gara, one of their shamans, recently fled the tribe, so meet me south of Hoelbrak and we'll see if we can all help each other. See you there, partner.—Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw

We've been here for... By the Wolf, days? Weeks? A month may have passed, but I don't have the slightest idea...    They killed Bakli, Magne's little boy. They dumped his body back into the cell. I...I covered him with my shawl. Closed his eyes. His feet were charred, his arms swollen and red. I haven't been taken for a round of experiments yet, but I fear for the worst when they do. It seems like only a few come back alive, and the others... I hope their deaths were swift, though I'm not ignorant to whatever reality awaits us deeper in the mine.    I can only pray to the spirits that Knut is sending aid.

We've been out here a handful of days, but I can tell Castora is something special. The other norn feel it; I wager even the sylvari feel it, too, in their own way. Nature is mostly unbound here, even with the presence of the castaways—wish my daughter could've joined me out here for a hunt or two.    I suspect there are many great marks, fierce beasts to witness my prowess. I'll ask around—maybe the locals can put me on the path of something unique, something worthy of my legend, or least, something the folks back home haven't seen before.    For now, I'm taking in the sights, the smells, the sounds. I feel as though I can hear a cacophony of wild spirits—spirits who're yet unaware that I am listening. I can hardly believe it! I will commune with them in time—make my presence known. To befriend a new spirit would be worth the trip by itself.

We've got another mission, Crusader: the quaggans in Lake Wyrmblood have been disappearing, and it looks like the dragons' minions are involved. The Vigil wants this seen to, so meet me there and we'll sort out whatever is preying on those soft-bodied, soft-headed pacifists.—Warmaster Forgal Kernsson

We've got the item secured and we're preparing to make the trek back to Supra Fortis. However, we've sustained significant equipment losses getting through the thick vines and the seer defenses. Our remaining loadout is:3 golems, one with nonfunctioning weapons8 machetes, all in need of sharpening3 shield generators, none at full functionality14 rifles, all of which have become mud clogged and unreliable6 field engineers, also mud clogged and unreliableLet's hope this was worth it, if we even make it back alive.

We've gotten several flattering reports regarding your actions in the field. I've learned to believe even the outlandish when it comes to you.Our targets have been hit. This operation is marked as a success. Well done. Of course, you're encouraged to proactively seek and eliminate these threats to Tyria. We'll continue issuing intel as agents withdraw.—HSPS: This message won't self-destruct, but you'll burn it yourself if you know what's good for you.
We've made some headway on our lodestone research. I'd like to discuss it more with you. Please come find me in Lion's Arch as soon as you can.—Ela Makkay

We've reached the remnants of something curious—a cathedral of blood! Ruins, laced with White Mantle iconography. Tucked away in the forest and shielded by storm like a reverent whisper... Saul most certainly was here, and whatever this place once was, his reach was powerful. This mission was set by something greater than me. I was meant to walk this land.    While we set up camp, I'm sending Ulrich into the ruins to search for anything he can find. Anything that tells us what happened here.    ---    It seems my labors bear more fruit. Ulrich returned to my tent with a locked chest—worn from decades of solitude but sealed shut to protect whatever sat inside. I had him crack the latch, revealing the time-locked contents. "Excitement" isn't the right word. "Validation" might be. Town ordinances, letters, receipts, health records... These ruins were a village. A village that was turned into a haven of radical thought. And it was led by Saul's chosen.    They called it Gavril, and it meant nothing before Saul filled it with his people.
We've received reports of rifts appearing across this area.

We, the Inquest (seller), hereby transfer ownership of one (1) Electro-Field Wide-Area Shock Transference Battery, hereinafter referred to as "The Machine," to Jessica and Jimmy Hammerhart (purchaser) of Twisting Hollows, Shipwreck Strand, Castora. By signing this contract, purchaser agrees to the following conditions:    —Purchaser acknowledges that the Machine must be set to the exacting specifications detailed in the included manual* for proper operation.    —At buyer's request, the Machine was modified for use by only 2 operators.    —Operators must be positively or negatively charged depending on their locations.    —Teleporters were also included in the purchase price, to accommodate the lack of a full krewe.    —Purchaser has released seller from any liability for aforementioned modifications, as well as purchaser's disinterest in safety warnings, power scale and range limits, optimal operating parameters, negative effects of humidity, temperature, elevation, &c &c.    In return, purchaser has provided access to Twisting Hollows cavern system, a company of experienced freebooters, an undisclosed sum of gold, and agreed to "Blow some Hullgardeners apart. BOOM!"    *Waived by purchaser.

Double-click the Writ of Experience to consume it and immediately gain 5%% of the XP required to reach the next level.Be sure to check back tomorrow for another gift to help you in your adventures!—ArenaNet

Welcome back, %str1%!We are thrilled you have been exploring Tyria for a full week! We hope you are enjoying yourself.Attached to this message you will find a Celebration Booster that will grant you +100%% magic find for 1 hour. Magic find helps you have a better chance at finding high-quality items as loot from slain foes.The Celebration Booster will also grant you double experience and gold from kills and give you a 10 percent bonus to PvP and WvW reward track progress while it is active.Thank you for playing Guild Wars 2, and we look forward to hearing the legends of your journey.—ArenaNet

Welcome, friend,Thank you for purchasing a membership with the Eldvin Monastery Brew of the Month Club. Over the next year, we'll supply a collection of beers specifically brewed for each particular month by the Eldvin Monastery or other small breweries around Tyria. At the start of each month, after you visit a major city, expect a package to arrive containing that month's finely crafted brew. Members who collect each month's brew will be awarded with our special VIP package containing a selection of special items and a title that reflects the passion of brew savants. Additional samples may be purchased by visitors to Eldvin Monastery in Queensdale. Due to the exclusive nature of our club, only club members will be allowed to purchase for their brew collection!Good health and a glass for your thirst,—Desch Metz,Master Brewer, Eldvin Monastery

You've challenged Frostgorge Sound, a land known for its bitter cold and Jormag's icy grip, and stood victorious over your enemies.The trials of Orr, and battle with Zhaitan, await you. Be careful, and embrace your newfound strength.Good travels,—Tyrian Explorers Society

Well, his head's on straight. We wouldn't still be standing here if it wasn't.
Well, it should be enough to protect you from the ice magic here.

Well, looks like we've hit a dead end. The portal puzzle was a clever idea for an intelligence test—Blish really knows what he's doing with the portal tech. Unfortunately, it looks like the specimens aren't showing any special intellectual capacity. I wasn't the only one expecting positive results due to the Balthazar energy absorbed by Kralkatorrik.A note: I'm starting to worry about housing the Branded specimens here. It's not just that they're extremely aggressive. I think there's strategy involved in their behavior. The other day, it really seemed like they were trying to trick me into leaving my notes in their lockup—notes that include the codes to the locks. Am I being paranoid?A thought: maybe we should be testing their strategic/tactical capacities rather than their intellect?
Well, thank you for the compliment. But this time I'm not after a dragon—I'm after Balthazar.

Well, well. You've certainly earned my attention, %str1%.What do they call you...the commander, was it? What a prestigious title. I've decided to make a special side project out of you, my dear. We're going to be seeing much more of each other, so you might as well cozy up and listen.I warned you not to get in my way, but here we are. Next time I won't be quite so...gentle.I simply can't wait to take you apart and see what makes you tick.Tick-tock-tick.—Scarlet (I don't need to hide behind silly codenames like "B" anymore, do I? This is quite exciting...)

Well, you've earned as much as I can pay you. Why don't you stop by to collect your reward? If you're looking for more work, I suspect you'll come in handy on the Diessa Plateau. We have real problems out on the range.—Maxtar Rapidstep, Bovaris Estate

What are alliances?Alliances allow multiple guilds to join forces when battling in the Mists. Allied guilds will be put together during matchmaking, allowing them to play in the same worlds.• When creating or joining an alliance, your guild will be invited to that alliance.• If your guild has more players than the allowed invite capacity, you will need to give permission to guild members to choose your guild as their WvW guild.• A guild may only belong to one alliance at a time.• An alliance is limited to 500 players. Who can create or accept alliance invitations?Guild leaders and those they give permission to may create an alliance or accept an alliance invitation. What are the requirements for joining an alliance?• You must meet the general criteria for participating in WvW events.• You must have selected a WvW guild.• You must have sufficient Badges of Honor to pay the creation fee to create (but not join) an alliance.

What happened out there? While you were grandstanding with Rytlock Brimstone, the members of our warband that you left behind suffered near-total losses. This mess is all your fault!I'm in charge, and I'm taking steps to replenish our numbers. If you expect to have a future in the Blood Legion, report to me in the Black Citadel immediately.—Legionnaire Urvan Steelbane

What the Forged are doing to these people is horrible. They're turning them into war machines against their will to continue Balthazar's nonsense quest. I'm glad reality didn't play out like this, for a few reasons.  These people just want to live their lives without conflict, not become meat bags in some warmonger's never-ending debacle. Even worse, their despair is starting to attract Kryptis left and right; they consume emotion, so of course, the fractals are ripe with confusion and pain. And even more are coming in from Nayos each day. I have a horrible feeling about what this place might become... The fractals are blood in water.  I know the commander beat Balthazar (in our reality, at least), but seeing what could've been is...a lot. There would be so much suffering without you. Wherever you are, I hope you know that. And I kinda wish you were here.

What would today constitute Reiko Murakami's rise to power began materializing during the year 1073 AE. It was during this time that Reiko, aided by her nephew Ashu, tapped into the fears of the Canthan public consciousness. And what was ailing the nation at this time was, of course, a very serious, very threatening public health crisis: the Affliction.  Using her outspoken position on the Affliction and its negative runoff into Canthan society, Reiko was able to garner increasing support, solidifying both Reiko and Ashu as public figures. It's no coincidence that at about this same time, Reiko began ascending the ranks of the Ministry of Flame. However, her time with the ministry would be ultimately short lived. She grew more vocal and disillusioned by what she deemed various flaws plaguing the organization. Citing various grievances ranging from "bureaucratic ineptitude" to "complete negligence of national welfare," Reiko concluded that a new ministry was needed to right the wrongs of the previous establishment.  And in 1077 AE, Reiko brought this very dream to fruition: she established the Ministry of Purity.

When Balthazar first began his conquest, it was assumed that Palawa Joko and his Awakened would rise to meet him. Then, when the commander failed, Elona's hopes fell on her usual tormentor, hoping that Joko could at least succeed in stopping the war god's rampage. But as more and more Awakened territory falls to the Forged, new doubts about the lich lord have been raised.    Joko's absence has been rationalized by three theories. First, the theory of his loyalists: that Joko is laying low to plan a crucial counterattack. Second, the theory of his enemies: that he has made a deal with Balthazar, selling out his people to protect his own life. But the third, growing in popularity every day, is that even the immortal Joko has somehow been killed by Balthazar. Opinions predictably vary about whether that would be a good thing.

When the grawl attacked Annerly's caravan I wasn't quite sure how I was going to recover her stolen goods. Your willingness to help a stranger in need is welcoming in these trying times. Annerly would send you thanks as well, but she's too busy attempting to rebuild her wagon. She hides her emotions well under that stubborn exterior. Anyway, be safe out there, and I hope our paths cross again in the future.—Glynis

When the Great Destroyer threatened to turn its ire on the free peoples of Tyria, no single race stood alone in the conflict that followed, and no single asuran challenged the evil of this tragic era more so than Vekk.Follow his journey in this comprehensive volume, from his beginnings as a simple elementalist to the indelible legacy he left as one of Tyria's greatest heroes.Praise for "The Genius of Vekk":"Combining written accounts from the era along with the most recent historical findings, this incredible biography covers everything from Vekk's knowledge of Scrying Pools to the emergence of asura gates in Tyria. I came away astounded at the depth and detail presented, and I would recommend this biography to anyone with an interest in the period."—Arcanist Timble, Durmand Priory

When we first begin to ask ourselves about the grand mysteries of the astral planes, we recognize with immediacy that we are no longer speaking about the physical world—although to the denizens of these biomes, they are as **natural** as the great stand of trees that lies in the heart of the forest are to us.    The key distinction between the biomes of the astral world and those of our own mundane one can ofttimes be **obscure** and flummox even the most logical of minds. We can compare it to the act of seeing a world behind the one we know, as though it is hiding from those who seek to ascend to new heights.    To truly understand the subject at hand, we must recognize our place in the great cosmic order first. If we hope to find our place in the **celestial** order, we must begin at the bottom—like the firm foundation of a great tower, stretching skyward.    Our next goal, then, should be to seek **balance**, so that as we expand our mind and our wisdom to accept more of the world beyond our own, we always have a place to center ourselves physically and mentally. We should plant ourselves in the place from which one can see both perspectives equally.    We will also need to attain an investiture of **strength** to stand firm against the many forces which would align against such efforts, looking to those who have led the way to guide our steps. There are those who have become paragons of their kind, and from them we learn how to be resolute.       Finally, as it is ultimately **knowledge** that we seek, we must immerse ourselves in the guiding words of those who have come before us. The wit and wisdom of our forebears travel through time from the past—captured in ink and on the page—to our present moment.
When you activate a signet, you gain a fire shield.

When you are training a young hunter, you must never make the prey too easy or the hunt too quick. They must not learn that they hunt solely for the reward, for the taking of a prize, for then they will use any means available and dishonor themselves as they do. Instead, time must be spent to allow them to fail, to follow the wrong path so that they will feel joy on rediscovering tracks, to miss their first arrow so that they will be driven to not miss their second. The hunt, like all things, is in the process, in yearning to become better. The real quarry is oneself, and that hunt has no end.
